title: havardia platyloba
intro: Havardia platyloba, also known as the Mexican copal tree or copal, is a deciduous tree native to Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Havardia platyloba include relief from fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to treat wounds and skin conditions. Traditionally, the tree's bark and leaves are used to prepare herbal remedies, which can be made into teas, infusions, or salves.
